slides.title: A few jumps a day can *rebuild* your bones.
slides.sub: Short bursts of impact send a signal that tells bone to grow stronger. The science calls it osteogenic loading.

slides.title: Impact is a signal
slides.body: Bone is living tissue that responds to stress. A hard landing tells it to *lay down new mineral* and reinforce itself.

slides.title: Loading builds density
slides.body: Quick, forceful jumps create the strain that *can support* bone remodeling far better than slow, steady movement alone.

slides.title: Stronger, harder to break
slides.body: Denser bone is more resistant to fractures. Plyometric training is linked to *better hip and spine* bone health.

slides.title: Short bouts are enough
slides.body: Just a few sets of jumps, a handful of times a week, *may help* stimulate bone without long, draining workouts.

slides.title: Build up your landings
slides.body: Begin with small hops and soft landings, then progress. Consistency over weeks is what *moves the needle*.

slides.title: Make every jump *count*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your movement, steps, sleep and food, then turns them into one daily Health Score so good habits stick.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
You don't need an hour at the gym to protect your bones. A few jumps a day can do real work. 🦴🌿

Impact loading signals bone to rebuild mineral density, build it up slowly with short bouts a few times a week. Save this. 🔖
